Manasseh was only half of the tribe of Joseph, yet it was divided into two parts. The daughters of Zelophehad now received the benefit of their pious zeal and prudent planning. Those who take care in this world to secure a place for themselves in the inheritance of the saints in light will have the comfort of it in the other world; while those who neglect it now will lose it forever. Lord, teach us here to believe and obey, and give us an inheritance among your saints, in everlasting glory.

There was great communication between Manasseh and Ephraim. Though each tribe had its inheritance, they should mix with one another, to do good deeds for one another, as became those who, though of different tribes, were all one Israel, and were bound to love as brothers. But they allowed the Canaanites to live among them, against God’s command, to serve their own purposes.

Joshua, as a public figure, had no more regard for his own tribe than for any other, but would govern without favoritism or affection; in this he has left a good example for all in public positions. Joshua tells them that what fell to their share would be enough for them if they would only work and fight. People excuse themselves from labor by any pretext; and nothing serves the purpose better than having rich and powerful relatives, able to provide for them; and they are apt to desire a partial and unfaithful distribution of what is entrusted to those they think able to give such help. But there is more real kindness in pointing out the advantages within reach, and in encouraging people to make the best of them, than in granting indulgences to laziness and extravagance. True religion gives no encouragement to these evils. The rule is, They shall not eat who will not work; and many of our “cannots” are only the language of idleness, which magnifies every difficulty and danger. This is especially the case in our spiritual work and warfare. Without Christ we can do nothing, but we are apt to sit still and attempt nothing. If we belong to Him, he will stir us up to our best endeavors, and to cry to him for help. Then our coast will be enlarged, and complainings silenced, or rather, turned into joyful thanksgivings.
